description: Every claim the tool or its agent makes that something is verified or tested names the runtime it was checked against, and never implies verification on a deployed runtime that did not happen, so the owner can tell what was actually proven.
iWant: every claim that something was verified to say what it was verified against
soThat: I am not told the deployed app works when only a local emulator was checked
title: Verification claims name their runtime

acceptanceCriteria.description: Every verified or tested claim names the runtime it was checked against
acceptanceCriteria.given: the shipped build guidance on reporting verification
acceptanceCriteria.then: the guidance requires the claim to name the runtime it was checked against

acceptanceCriteria.description: No claim implies deploy-runtime verification that did not happen
acceptanceCriteria.given: a verification that ran against a local or emulated runtime
acceptanceCriteria.then: the guidance forbids the claim from stating or implying verification on a deployed runtime that was not exercised

acceptanceCriteria.description: The PR-authoring guidance requires the runtime label in the verification section
acceptanceCriteria.when: the verification section of a PR body is authored
acceptanceCriteria.then: it requires each verification claim to carry the runtime it was checked against

acceptanceCriteria.description: The guidance includes an explicit labelled-claim example
acceptanceCriteria.then: it includes an explicit example naming the runtime (for example 'verified against wrangler dev (localhost) - NOT the deployed Worker runtime')
